Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants a Python dictionary representing a DECLARE model based on a specific scenario a complex multi department product design and launch process Analyze the Input Data Format A Python dictionary Structure Keys Constraint Types existence absence exactly one init responded existence coexistence response precedence succession altresponse altprecedence altsuccession chainresponse chainprecedence chainsuccession noncoexistence nonsuccession nonchainsuccession Scenario Activities IG Idea Generation DD Design Draft TFC Technical Feasibility Check CE Cost Evaluation PC Prototype Creation LT Laboratory Testing UT User Testing AG Approval Gate MP Marketing Plan FL Final Launch

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Determine Logic Constraints The Story Start Usually starts with Idea Generation IG So init should probably be IG Existence All these activities likely happen at least once in a successful process instance Sequence Flow IG DD Idea Draft DD TFC CE Draft needs feasibility and cost check precedence TFC PC precedence CE PC TFC CE AG Need checks before approval Specific Constraints Init IG Response If A then eventually B If DD then eventually PC If PC then eventually FL If AG Final Approval then FL Precedence B requires A happened before DD requires IG TFC requires DD CE requires DD PC requires TFC CE AG LT requires PC UT requires PC MP requires AG FL requires LT UT MP Coexistence TFC and CE often go together LT and UT often go together

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Refining the Constraints for the Dictionary Initialization Start with IG Core Flow IG DD DD TFC and or CE TFC AG Design Check Approval AG PC PC LT UT LT AG Final Gate Or maybe the prompt implies only one AG Assume AG is the gateway to Prototyping After PC we test LT UT Before FL we need MP select a robust subset of constraints to demonstrate the dictionary structure effectively init IG existence All activities response IG DD DD TFC DD CE TFC AG CE AG AG PC PC LT PC UT LT FL UT FL AG MP precedence IG DD DD TFC DD CE TFC AG CE AG AG PC PC LT PC UT MP FL coexistence TFC CE LT UT

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Formatting the Output Must be valid Python syntax dictionary Keys must match exactly the list provided in the prompt Values must be dictionaries Unary keys map activity tuples Act support confidence Binary keys map A B support confidence Reviewing the Prompt Specifics For the keys existence absence exactly one init the value is a dictionary containing as keys the activities For the keys responded existence the value is a dictionary containing as keys the activities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Constructing the Content Activities IG DD TFC CE PC LT UT AG MP FL Drafting the Dict init IG existence IG DD TFC CE PC LT UT AG MP FL response IG DD DD TFC DD CE AG PC PC LT PC UT AG MP LT FL UT FL MP FL precedence IG DD DD TFC DD CE TFC AG CE AG AG PC PC LT PC UT MP FL coexistence TFC CE LT UT

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish Ensure the keys match the prompts required list exactly Ensure values are formatted correctly tuples inside dict Add comments explaining the logic Self Correction on Key Format In pm4py specifically for existence etc the key is usually the activity name string For relations it is a tuple A B I will follow this convention Also the prompt mentions support and confidence as values I will default to 10 for both as this is a designed model not mined from noisy logs with thresholds Checking Constraint Logic against Scenario IG DD Makes Sense DD TFC CE Parallel checks TFC CE AG Approval gate AG PC Prototype PC LT UT Testing LT UT FL Launch Implicitly passing MP Marketing AG MP Response and MP FL Precedence works well

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Note The prompt says as keys the activities for binary ones too but in standard DECLARE pm4py implementations binary constraints map pairs of activities to values The prompt text is slightly ambiguous as keys the activities but looking at standard pm4py DECLARE format binary constraints use A B tuples as keys I will assume the standard pm4py tuple format for binary constraints because mapping just an activity name for a binary constraint makes no sense semantically
